hooks.command: file_path=$(echo "$TOOL_INPUT" | jq -r '.file_path // empty'); if [ -n "$file_path" ] && echo "$file_path" | grep -q 'paper/paper\.typ$'; then cd "$(dirname "$file_path")/.." && typst compile --root . paper/paper.typ 2>&1 | tail -5; fi

file_path=$(echo "$TOOL_INPUT" | jq -r '.file_path // empty'); if [ -n "$file_path" ] && echo "$file_path" | grep -q 'ref\.bib$'; then echo 'BLOCKED: ref.bib is auto-exported from Zotero via BBT. Use .claude/scripts/zotero_add.py to add citations.' >&2; exit 2; fi

file_path=$(echo "$TOOL_INPUT" | jq -r '.file_path // empty'); if [ -n "$file_path" ] && echo "$file_path" | grep -qE 'analysis/output/(results|figures)/'; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"additionalContext":"WARNING: Output files should be regenerated by R scripts, not manually edited. If you are intentionally creating/updating this file from script output, proceed."}}'; fi

echo '{"systemMessage":"CRITICAL POST-COMPACTION REMINDERS: (1) After ANY edit to paper/paper.typ, run typst compile --root . paper/paper.typ. (2) Never edit ref.bib directly — use Zotero. (3) Never modify raw data in analysis/data/. (4) Always verify before claiming done. (5) Use opus model for all agents."}'

echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"UserPromptSubmit","additionalContext":"SESSION INIT: Before starting work, orient yourself: (1) Read revision/todo.md for pending tasks. (2) Review .claude/lessons.md for learned patterns. (3) Check recent git history if relevant. This prevents re-discovering state and wasting tokens.\n\nSESSION NAMING: After completing your response to this first prompt, end with a /rename suggestion. Pick a short name (3-5 words, no quotes) that captures the session topic. Format: /rename short-name-here"}}'
